items.content: The Future of Layout
Our new layout engine brings professional-grade, multi-column typesetting directly into the automated document pipeline.
The magazine's equipo editorial verifies every proof before publication, ensuring typographic fidelity is maintained across all scripts, locales, and output formats throughout the production cycle.
Each rubrique de service is composited in its native locale, letting the engine demonstrate its multilingual shaping, kerning, and bidirectional text capabilities across diverse glyph sets.

This is the start of our two-column layout. When you work with advanced automated layouts, handling multi-column text that effortlessly snaps around floating obstacles is crucial for that high-end magazine feel. We need the layout to show line-width variance from obstacle wrapping, so we push the text carefully around it.

It allows us to place elements visually without breaking the narrative flow. We can even mix languages naturally: これ는テストです. El veloz murciélago comía feliz cardillo y kiwi. And the text wraps flawlessly around our floating obstacle. This text should wrap right next to the obstacle, forcing line width variance in the two-column format! Yes, the obstacle forces the text to wrap tightly, reducing the line width and satisfying the variance check.

We need A LOT of text to force three pages. Let's just repeat a long block of text. To push the text down and fill the page securely so we spill naturally over or hit page bounds, let's include an abundant amount of text to test the capabilities of the pagination system in a multicolumn scenario. We want to ensure that it correctly handles columns spanning up to the bottom margin and continuing seamlessly on the right column. It needs to look refined, elegant, and purposeful — exactly as it would in a modern digital magazine, newspaper, or academic journal where multi-column layouts are the standard. The text wraps flawlessly around obstacles. We continue generating text.

A Densely Packed Three-Column Analysis
Three columns are challenging because the line length becomes very narrow, making hyphenation and justification engines work aggressively. We are testing how well text shapes around tight obstacles.

In a three-column setup, every pixel counts. Center-aligned floats carve out chunks of an already narrow span. We simulate a stylized text box here representing an author picture, small graphic, or callout. This text must wrap around the center float, forcing lines to split left and right of the obstacle!

Edge Case: Multi-column Lists
We can use columns for more than large pages of texts. Consider an index, or a list of items packed efficiently:
1. Apples
2. Bananas
3. Cherries
4. Dates
5. Elderberry
6. Figs
7. Grapes
8. Honeydew
9. Iceberg
10. Jackfruit
